title: polemonium boreale subsp. richardsonii
intro: Polemonium boreale subsp. richardsonii, also known as Richardson's Jacob's-ladder or Richardson's polemonium, is a plant species that belongs to the family Polemoniaceae and is native to the western regions of North America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of P. boreale subsp. richardsonii include its ability to reduce inflammation, lower blood pressure, and act as an antispasmodic, providing relief from muscle cramps and spasms. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its dried leaves and roots, which can be infused into teas or made into tinctures and salves for topical application.
